summary: Frontend-only performance pass for the dashboard-instant-render + optimistic-delete optimisations. Mobile viewport 390x844, web preview, Firebase login as testuser+pv@example.com. Seeded 3 cards (Pikachu/Charizard/Mewtwo) via direct POST /api/portfolio/save for uid ZhopI1m5jeQJr5MBoZbHPXqAFtH3. VERIFIED: (1) INSTANT RENDER — on dashboard mount the 3 card rows + total card render immediately with persisted estimated_value (4,00 EUR each, total 12,00 EUR shown on first paint); no blocking spinner. (2) NON-BLOCKING REFOCUS — navigating Scan tab → Dashboard tab the card list remains visible the entire time; only 1 silent /api/portfolio GET fires, ZERO /api/price calls (fetchedSignaturesRef dedupe works perfectly). (3) OPTIMISTIC DELETE PARAM mechanism — pushed ?deletedId=<id> via history.pushState/popstate, dashboard's useEffect at dashboard.tsx:56-68 synchronously filtered the row out of local state in <2.5s (rows 3→2, total 12,00→8,00 EUR, count 3 cards saved→2 cards saved). NO DELETE call was fired by the dashboard (correct — DELETE is owned by card-detail). NO /api/price storm on the surviving cards from the in-app flow. (4) cards.length===0 gate on the blocking loader at dashboard.tsx:431 is correctly placed — loader only renders when the list is empty AND loading. LIMITATION (cannot test on web): the full card-detail → trash → confirm Alert.alert flow CANNOT be exercised on RN-web because react-native-web's Alert.alert maps multi-button alerts to window.alert (single OK) and never fires onPress on the destructive button. This is a platform-level RN-web limitation, NOT a regression in this iteration. The remove() handler in card-detail.tsx:158-177 will work correctly on iOS/Android. CRITICAL EDGE CASE FOUND: arriving at /dashboard?deletedId=X as the FIRST URL (direct link / browser reload while the param is in the URL) crashes with 'Uncaught Error: Attempted to navigate before mounting the Root Layout component' — the router.setParams({deletedId:''}) call at dashboard.tsx:66 runs in useEffect on first mount before the Root Layout has finished mounting on web. This does NOT affect the normal in-app card-detail → router.replace flow (Root Layout is already mounted), but a user reloading the page with the param still in the URL will see the red-screen error overlay. Recommended fix: defer the setParams call (queueMicrotask / setTimeout 0) or guard with a useRef flag.

ui_bugs.issue: Reloading /dashboard?deletedId=X (or following a deep link with that param) crashes with red-screen 'Uncaught Error: Attempted to navigate before mounting the Root Layout component' originating at dashboard.tsx:66 (router.setParams). Reproduce: in the web preview, log in, then page.goto('/dashboard?deletedId=anything') — the error overlay covers the screen. Reference: /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/dashboard.tsx:56-68.

react_native_issues.component: RN-web Alert.alert (platform limitation, not introduced this iteration)
react_native_issues.issue: react-native-web's Alert.alert only honours a single OK button — destructive/cancel onPress callbacks never fire on web. This means the card-detail trash → Remove flow CANNOT be E2E-tested via Playwright on the web preview. On iOS/Android the flow works as designed. Reference: /app/frontend/app/card-detail.tsx:158-177.

react_native_issues.component: shadow*/textShadow* deprecated props (pre-existing, carry-over from iter5/iter6)
react_native_issues.issue: RN-web still warns 'shadow*'/'textShadow*' style props are deprecated on first paint. Non-blocking. Reference: /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/dashboard.tsx:466-470 (totalValue textShadow*) and /app/frontend/app/card-detail.tsx:446,459 (estimateValue textShadow*, retentionMarker shadow*).

action_items: Fix the dashboard.tsx:66 router.setParams crash on first-mount with ?deletedId param. Wrap the setParams in a setTimeout(()=>router.setParams({deletedId:''}),0) or queueMicrotask, OR gate it behind a useRef flag that only fires after the first effect cycle. This guarantees the Root Layout has finished mounting before navigation methods are called.
(Optional) Migrate shadow*/textShadow* RN-web warnings (dashboard.tsx:466-470, card-detail.tsx:446,459) to boxShadow/textShadow strings to silence repeat warnings.

(Out of scope for web) Document in the codebase that the card-detail → trash → Remove flow is only fully exercisable on iOS/Android; the web preview shows the trash icon but the destructive Alert button is a no-op due to RN-web limitation. Could add a Platform.OS==='web' branch in remove() that bypasses Alert.alert and uses a custom inline confirm if web parity matters.
critical_code_review_comments: dashboard.tsx:56-68 — useEffect that consumes deletedIdParam and writes to pendingDeletes ref + filters state synchronously is correct in design. ONLY issue: router.setParams at line 66 runs unconditionally on first mount, which crashes when deletedId is in the initial URL (Expo Router hasn't mounted Root Layout yet). Wrap in setTimeout(...,0) to defer past the mount tick.
dashboard.tsx:70-84 — load() filters out pendingDeletes-tagged ids defensively — this is the race-protection mechanism. Correct: race window is 4s (line 64).

dashboard.tsx:102-111 — useFocusEffect now only sets loading=true when cards.length===0. This is the key win: subsequent tab focuses run load() silently in the background while the existing list stays on screen. VERIFIED live: switching Scan→Dashboard kept the 3 rows visible the whole time with zero spinner flash.

dashboard.tsx:166-204 — live-price effect uses fetchedSignaturesRef Set to dedupe by id|name|set|number signature, fires individual api.getPrice calls (no Promise.all), patches state per-card on resolve, and removes the signature on failure to allow retry. VERIFIED: refocus produced 0 /api/price calls (3 surviving signatures all cached).

card-detail.tsx:158-177 — remove() correctly fire-and-forgets api.deleteCard and immediately router.replace's to /(tabs)/dashboard with deletedId param. NOT testable on web due to RN Alert limitation, but design is sound.
card-detail.tsx:60-80 — saved-mode live-price refetch is independent of the dashboard flow and unaffected by this iteration's changes.
src/api.ts:102-103 — deleteCard request is unchanged; backend DELETE /api/portfolio/{id} contract preserved.

success_rate.frontend: 5/6 testable assertions PASS (instant render, non-blocking refocus, no /api/price storm, cached signatures, optimistic deletedId filter+race protection). 1/6 cannot be tested on web (full card-detail Alert flow) — platform limitation only. 1 NEW edge-case bug found (router.setParams crash on first-mount with deletedId in URL).

seed_data_creation: Seeded 3 cards (Pikachu/Charizard/Mewtwo, all Base #1) for uid ZhopI1m5jeQJr5MBoZbHPXqAFtH3 via direct POST /api/portfolio/save. price_at_creation=5.0, condition_multiplier=0.8, estimated_value=4.0 each. Backend still has all 3 at end of run (Mewtwo was deleted during one test then restored). To clean: DELETE /api/portfolio/{id} for the 3 ids: 6dd7cfef-4ec8-4e4e-95ad-9a2c0ee3a1cc (Mewtwo), dd9ce753-0050-4523-9e46-c88a7d2a42a4 (Charizard), 75381eef-a480-46e9-9994-5bd60fc511a5 (Pikachu).
context_for_next_testing_agent: (1) The dashboard now caches /api/price signatures in fetchedSignaturesRef and patches state per-card. To assert no-storm: monitor network for /api/price requests, then trigger a tab refocus or an optimistic deletedId update — expected count is 0 for the cached cards. (2) Optimistic delete cannot be tested via the trash button on web (RN-web Alert.alert single-OK limitation). Use this workaround to test the dashboard's deletedId mechanism in isolation: page.evaluate to push history.pushState with ?deletedId=<id> and dispatch popstate — the dashboard's useEffect picks it up and filters the row synchronously. (3) When testing initial URL with ?deletedId=X (e.g. page.goto('/dashboard?deletedId=foo')), the page CURRENTLY crashes with 'Attempted to navigate before mounting the Root Layout' — this is the bug reported in this iteration. After the main agent fixes it (setTimeout 0 on router.setParams), retest. (4) The 3 seeded cards have no live /api/price match in pokemontcg.io (set_name='Base', number='1' is not a real card), so trend badges (testID='portfolio-trend-<id>') do NOT render — `live` resolves to null and the badge is gated by `live !== null`. To exercise trend badges, seed cards with real Pokemon names+sets (e.g. set_name='Base Set', number='4' for Charizard).
rca_of_issue: EDGE-CASE BUG: dashboard.tsx:66 calls router.setParams({deletedId:''}) inside the useEffect that consumes deletedIdParam. When the URL initially contains ?deletedId=X (browser refresh / deep link / direct navigation), the useEffect runs on first render — BEFORE Expo Router's Root Layout has finished mounting on web. The setParams call throws 'Attempted to navigate before mounting the Root Layout component'. Reproduce: from the dashboard, page.goto('/dashboard?deletedId=anything') — red-screen overlay appears. FIX: defer the setParams call by one tick using `setTimeout(() => router.setParams({deletedId:''}), 0)` or `queueMicrotask(() => router.setParams(...))`. This guarantees the navigator hierarchy has mounted before the param-clear runs. The in-app flow (card-detail router.replace → dashboard) is unaffected because the Root Layout is already mounted in that case.
